relevant_files.role: SecurityScanner implementation - orchestrates diff/file scanning, calls PatternEngine
PatternEngine - regex-based line-by-line scanning with ignore rules
14 security patterns (hardcoded-secret, sql-injection, path-traversal, command-injection, xss, insecure-random, weak-crypto, unsafe-deserialization)
Finding, PatternDefinition, ValidatedFinding, Severity, Confidence types
needs_security_scan - determines when to trigger security scans based on context
Integration tests including test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected documenting current limitation
Workspace config - deps, lints, edition 2024, rust 1.92.0

conventions.commits: Conventional commits, GPG signed, DCO signoff required
conventions.testing: Unit tests in modules (mod tests), integration tests in tests/, benchmarks in benches/
conventions.linting: clippy with pedantic, rustfmt, deny unused_must_use
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications

patterns: Regex-based line-by-line pattern matching in PatternEngine
PatternDefinition with regex, severity, confidence, CWE, file_extensions fields
SecurityScanner.scan_diff() only scans added lines in unified diffs
Current architecture cannot detect multi-line vulnerabilities (source/sink on different lines)

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Main issue - proposes evaluating tree-sitter to replace/enhance regex-based scanning for multi-line vulnerability detection

related_issues.title: Add negative test documenting multi-line vulnerability detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Documents architectural limitation that tree-sitter would solve

related_issues.title: Add test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected
related_issues.relevance: Merged PR - adds test documenting that current scanner cannot detect multi-line SQL injection

constraints: Current scanner is line-by-line only - cannot detect multi-line vulnerabilities
14 patterns would need conversion or AST-aware equivalents
Language grammar crates needed for each supported language
test_coverage: Integration tests cover: hardcoded_secrets.rs (API keys, passwords), sql_injection.rs, command injection, weak crypto (MD5/SHA1), safe patterns. Includes test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected documenting known limitation. Unit tests in detection.rs cover label/description/path triggers.

library_findings.version: latest (C library, Rust bindings via tree-sitter crate)
library_findings.notes: Incremental parsing, syntax tree traversal, capture groups for queries

library_findings.relevant_api: language() function returns Language for Rust grammar
library_findings.notes: Official grammar for Rust - enables AST queries on .rs files, useful for detecting Rust-specific vulnerabilities
Useful for SQL injection detection in SQL files or embedded SQL

approaches.name: Hybrid: Tree-sitter for SQL injection only
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-rust and tree-sitter-sql as optional deps. Create new SqlInjectionScanner using AST queries. Keep existing regex scanner for other patterns. Minimal risk, proves concept.
pros: Low risk - isolated to new module
Proven pattern in other tools (Semgrep, CodeQL)
cons: SQL injection is only one pattern
Maintenance burden of two scanning approaches
May need more grammar crates for full coverage

approaches.name: Full AST rewrite
approaches.description: Replace PatternEngine entirely with tree-sitter-based ASTQueryEngine. Convert all 14 patterns to tree-sitter queries (S-expression syntax). Implement multi-language support.
pros: More precise than regex (false positive reduction)

approaches.name: Layered: Regex fallback with AST enhancement
approaches.description: Keep existing PatternEngine as base. Add AST layer on top for patterns that benefit (SQL injection, command injection, unsafe deserialization). Use tree-sitter for multi-line detection, regex for single-line.
cons: Pattern authors need both regex and query knowledge
Unclear which patterns benefit most without research

recommendation: Approach 1 (Hybrid) - Start with tree-sitter-rust for SQL injection detection as spike. This directly addresses #737's suggested approach, minimizes risk, and provides concrete learnings for expanding to other patterns. The test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected in the codebase already documents the limitation this solves. After the spike, evaluate whether to expand AST coverage or proceed to Approach 2.
